observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to send notices to the payees concerned on the existence of the checks that had become stale, otherwise require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate reversion to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund of the AP that have been outstanding for at least two years and which could not be supported with complete documentation; and

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the necessary documents to prove the validity of the recorded AP, otherwise make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to fairly present the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The notices were sent to the respective payees, however, the Municipality has not received responses yet. In addition to the notices, some payees were reminded verbally about their stale checks, but some of them opted not to collect after they assessed that their receivables amounts were immaterial, and hence, subject APs will be reverted accordingly. Moreover, claims totaling P35,324.98 were recorded as AP at year-end despite the absence of proof of actual deliveries of goods or services, these claims could not be supported with complete documentation. The necessary documents proving the validity of the APs were submitted to the Audit Team on May 14, 2024.
observations.reason: The Municipality opted to give the payees a chance to respond and produce the original copy of stale check, which will be their basis for reprocessing the payments. On the other hand, the necessary documents proving the validity of the APs were submitted to the Audit Team on May 14, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Direct its authorized official to coordinate with the FDA to identify the requirements needed to facilitate issuance of LTO/CFR/other related authorization that will enable the radiation facilities of the Mobile Clinic to become operational; and

b. Ensure careful and thorough planning, feasibility study and assessment of necessary legal and technical requirements are conducted before spending or investing in a project, especially if the funding thereof will be sourced from bank loans with huge interest payments, to avoid wastage of government resources and ensure that optimal benefits from the project are enjoyed by the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Office coordinated with FDA and other concerned agencies; the Municipal Mayor’s Office, Municipal Administrator and Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) ensured necessary feasibility studies were conducted for projects implemented in CY 2024. The Municipal Accountant prepared a Recommendation Tracking Sheet for the project. However, manpower requirements remained an issue and the Municipality has been implementing measures to address this.
observations.reason: The Municipality failed to meet the manpower requirement to operate the X-ray laboratory, consisting of at least four professionals: radiologist, radiologic technician, facility head, and additional staff. Hence the Mobile Clinic remained underutilized; recommendation reiterated in Part II Observation No. 10.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to:

a. Facilitate the prompt submission of the lacking documents and/or correction of deficiencies to COA in order to prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions; and

b. Strictly comply with the documentary requirements of COA Circular No. 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023 and the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the conduct of public biddings and processing of payments of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat and Engineering Office submitted the lacking documents on June 18, 2024. Concerned officers were reminded to ensure completeness and proper safekeeping of all engineering and procurement documents.
observations.reason: While many lacking documents were submitted on June 18, 2024, some procurement transactions amounting to P20,723,068.33 were still not fully supported with required documents and the prescribed procurement processes were not strictly adhered to; hence some items remain not implemented/suspended pending submission of documents (reiterated in Part II Observation No. 11).

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year's recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Office (GSO) to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, including the determination of the appropriate mode of disposal and appraisal of the same to prevent further deterioration and reduction of the properties’ economic value.
observations.managementAction: An Inspection Report of unserviceable properties was submitted to the Audit Team. Some properties were inspected to confirm the determination of the appropriate mode of disposal. The Municipality reclassified some unserviceable properties and applied necessary adjustments; portions were disposed on May 23, 2024 for certain items with an aggregated cost.
observations.reason: Disposal process could not be fully completed by December 31, 2024 due to ongoing documentary and procedural requirements; portions were disposed while other remaining properties were reclassified or await disposal procedures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Health Officer to obtain the necessary PTC and submit a copy to the COA Audit Team; and if PTC cannot be obtained, the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Administrator to find other uses for the building to avoid idle asset; and apply corrective measures to put it into use or determine the beneficial action.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted a written justification on May 31, 2024 explaining why construction began without PTC. Management made efforts to secure PTC since CY 2024. The LGU decided to repurpose the Dialysis Center into a primary care facility due to additional costs required to meet dialysis specifications. An unsolicited proposal for a proponent to operate a primary care facility was received; the proponent has already obtained the necessary PTC from DOH. Negotiations and lease agreement remain under review; approval from the Sangguniang Bayan is required.
observations.reason: Management undertook steps and submitted justification and engaged in negotiations with a proponent who has secured PTC; however, the repurposed facility was not yet operational as of report time and lease/approval processes remained pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the SWMB to establish in each Barangay or cluster of Barangays a MRF taking into consideration the prescribed layout/design for DENR-EMB funded MRF and provide additional support to Barangays to comply with Section 32 of RA No. 9003; and that the LCE convene the SWMB to determine the nature of additional support to the MRFs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal LGU coordinated with DENR-EMB and conducted information campaigns. The LGU is working to increase its budget for hauling services and to identify suitable government-owned lots to establish MRFs. Some barangays are reluctant due to land availability and logistics.
observations.reason: Main challenges include identification/acquisition of suitable lots and logistics. Hence, MRF establishment in every barangay remained unimplemented as of reporting.

observations.recommendation:

2.8.b. The Finance Committee establish sound and actionable policies for
the collection of all past and present receivables.

perform the physical count of the Municipality’s PPE for reliable PPE

which were created from conversion of Old to the NGAS and/or from the manual

semi-expendable property, with an acquisition cost of less than P50,000.00 per
item in compliance with COA Circular No. 2022-004; and
b. OIC-General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the SPC for each class of
semi-expendable property and RegSPI for the issuance, return, reissue and

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
make the necessary adjustments to present fairly the balances of the affected
accounts in the SFPos. Thereafter, recognize the grants received from the
Provincial Government of Cavite and its subsequent utilization and liquidation
using the Due to LGUs as prescribed under the Revised Chart of Accounts (RCA)
for LGUs, Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare a complete Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the
end of the year and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant who, on the basis
thereof, shall prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the restoration of the cash
equivalent of the unreleased checks and recognition of the appropriate

ensuing year to fairly present the balances of the affected accounts in the SFPos; and
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare accounting entries to take up the cancelled and
stale checks and restoration of the CIB, Government Equity and appropriate
liability accounts, in accordance with Sections 43 and 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and
Municipal Accountant to immediately cause the submission of the noted lacking
documents to the COA Audit Team to facilitate further audit, evaluation and
technical review of the COA TAS in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001,
otherwise, the payment transactions covered by the subject infrastructure
projects will be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to
ensure that the project signboards and/or public notices are displayed at the
project location, and the information therein and in the QRGPPAs are complete, as
required under COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Engineer and Market Supervisor to explain to the COA Audit Team
and submit the project monitoring reports, or any relevant documentation
regarding the actual site conditions, to justify the non-completion of the Design and
Build of Alfonso Public Market (Phase 2) within the original contract time;
b. Municipal Engineer to formulate and implement an action plan to fast-track the
completion of the project costing P162,684,592.41 and thereafter, ensure strict
compliance with the guidelines on the issuance of contract extension provided under
the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to avoid unnecessary delay in the
implementation of infrastructure projects, otherwise, liquidated damages shall be
imposed against the contractor;
c. Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to ensure that necessary
detailed engineering activities are conducted prior to procurement of infrastructure
projects;
d. BAC to require the submission of certification from the Municipal Engineering
Office that detailed engineering surveys and designs have been conducted by the
latter; and
46
e. Municipal Accountant to require the contractor to submit a renewed surety bond
callable upon demand, issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by
the Insurance Commission and confirmed by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Engineer to submit the written request for payment of mobilization and an
irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a
bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand, issued by a surety or
insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission and confirmed by
the Municipality, in the equivalent amount of the unrecouped advance payments.
Otherwise, submit a written explanation why the same cannot be submitted for
evaluation of the Audit Team. Otherwise, the transactions will be suspended in
audit or any amount of the advance payments that may not be recovered by the
Municipality shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. OIC-General Services Officer to require the supplier to correct the defective
item and to deliver any lacking components. Otherwise, impose liquidated
damages and other sanctions against the supplier and require the BAC to explain
to the COA Audit Team, for further evaluation, of any changes/revision made in the
technical specification of the procurement; and
b. Municipal Health Officer and other responsible officials to explain to the COA
Audit Team, for further evaluation, on why the Mobile Clinic has not been used
and the specific actions taken and to be taken by them to ensure that the amount
of P23,978,000.00 is not wasted, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance (ND) will be
issued accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant,
Municipal Engineer and BAC to submit to the Commission on Audit (COA) Audit
Team for evaluation all the lacking eligibility requirements, minutes of required
procurement activities in compliance with the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, and the
other documents relative to the subject procurements, otherwise, the transactions
will be suspended in audit. Thereafter, the Municipal Engineer to prepare the
Detailed Computations of Contract Time and Statement of Work Accomplished
complete with essential details such as Quantity of Accomplishment and the
Amount of Accomplishment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Health Officer to obtain the necessary PTC and submit a copy to the
COA Audit Team for evaluation;
b. Municipal Administrator, and other responsible officials to make decisive
actions and fast-track the review of the project proposal. Thereafter, submit a copy
of the documents relative to the negotiations to the COA Audit Team, for further
51

evaluation; and
c. Municipal Health Officer, Municipal Administrator, and other responsible
officials to provide to the COA Audit Team the actions taken and to be taken by
them to ensure the immediate use and utilization of the project as intended, and
the amount of P10,788,453.45 is not wasted, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance
(ND) will be issued accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer, Market
Supervisor, and other responsible officials to explain to the COA Audit Team, for
further evaluation: (a) the reasons for not obtaining authorization from the SB prior to
establishing the Flea Market; and (b) the actions taken and to be taken by the
Municipality to ensure the immediate use and utilization of the project as intended,
and the amount of P8,007,164.90 is not wasted, otherwise, a ND will be issued
accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer:
a. Together with other responsible officials to submit an explanation for the noted
deficiency and coordinate with the contractor to refund the excessive payment of
P189,011.98, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance (ND) will be issued by the Audit
Team; and
b. To meticulously and judiciously prepare the ABC in accordance with the pertinent
provisions of DPWH Department Order No. 197 dated October 7, 2016 in order to
prevent miscalculations which could result in excessive expenditures on
infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

a. The Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and request reports that will provide the status of the infrastructure projects implemented at year-end;

b. The Municipal Engineer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the relevant reports to ascertain the status and proper recording of all government program, project, or activities (PPAs); and

c. The Municipal Accountant to prepare correcting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts by crediting CIP and AP accounts by P1,702,748.82 and P189,194.31, respectively; and debiting the PPE account by P1,891,943.13.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant requested the Municipal Engineer to furnish them with the reports needed to properly record the projects implemented at year-end.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustment to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts per Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 101-2024-05-49725 dated May 14, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to request, in writing, the retrieval of necessary supporting documents from the bank which will serve as a basis in the recording of reconciling items; and

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare adjusting journal entries for all valid reconciling items identified during the process of bank reconciliation to present the actual balances of accounts as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were made per JEV No. 100-2024-05-50007 and JEV No. 100-2024-05-49707. The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the bank manager to retrieve the transaction history and the origin of the amount credited and which debited in its account. Several debit memos reflected in the bank reconciling items were proven to be interest expenses. Adjusting entries were made to correct the cash understatement recorded as P41.65 under reference.
observations.reason: Adjusting entries were made and the Municipal Accountant coordinated with the bank to retrieve necessary documents; entries were processed per JEV Nos. cited.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Secretariat post the NOA for procurement projects within three calendar days from its issuance on the PhilGEPS website and require the Procurement Officer or the official responsible for accepting deliveries of procured items to submit the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) to the COA within 24 hours of delivery in compliance with COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat posted the NOA within the required timeline. The General Services Office (GSO) submitted soft copies of the AIR via email within 24 hours of delivery acceptance, while hard copies were submitted to the COA Office every Friday.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure the timely release of the barangays’ shares from RPT collections in compliance with Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Municipal Accountant ensured the timely release of RPT to the barangays each quarter throughout CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and other municipal officials concerned to adhere to the provisions in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 on warranty security, either by deducting retention money from payments or requiring a special bank guarantee, and that all suppliers submit warranty security for their payments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office required the suppliers to submit warranty security and/or hold at least five percent retention on their payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to require every Accountable Officer (AO) who will receive a cash advance to strictly observe the prescribed due date for the liquidation of his/her cash advance and to ensure that all cash advances are duly liquidated and/or refunded at the end of each year.
observations.managementAction: Liquidations of cash advances were made within the prescribed period and the Municipal Accountant required accountable officers to observe prescribed liquidation schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Market Administrator to post Notices of Vacant or Newly Constructed Stall in the prescribed areas; execute Contracts of Lease between the lessees and the Municipality in the presence of the Market Committee; and ensure judicious planning before undertaking new infrastructure projects, including appropriate utilization planning of available spaces.
observations.managementAction: Notices of Vacant or Newly Constructed Stalls were posted in the prescribed areas. The copies of the executed Contracts of Lease were submitted to COA on June 14, 2024. The Municipality committed to improved planning.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to submit to the Office of the Auditor the Monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO and MDRRMC/Municipal Development Council (MDC) submitted the Monthly Reports of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the Municipal SWMB is able to perform its duties to assess the sustainability, viability and effectiveness of the Municipality’s SWMP, and that the Municipal SWMB conduct its biennial review as required.
observations.managementAction: The SWMB was organized and convened (organized on September 12, 2023) and conducted reviews and activities to prioritize sustainability and viability. For CY 2024, the Municipal SWMB performed duties as outlined in Section 12 of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, including appraisal and the appropriate mode of disposal to prevent deterioration and reduction of economic value.
observations.managementAction: Disposal was made on May 23, 2024 for certain unserviceable equipment with aggregated cost disclosed; remaining properties were reclassified and adjustments applied; some properties were disposed while others are awaiting completion of disposal requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and Head, Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) submit documents such as Personal Data Sheets (PDS), Certificates of Training or equivalent documents that will prove the competence and qualifications of the 60 workers; and documents detailing their functions and responsibilities, deliverables and actual work accomplishments signed by immediate supervisors, otherwise payments to them shall be suspended and/or disallowed.
observations.managementAction: The required documents including PDS and certificates of training and documents detailing functions and deliverables were submitted to COA on May 24, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct HRMO, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer and other municipal offices, to study manpower needs and ensure hiring of JO workers is based on specific identified piece of work; terminating redundant JO workers whose job descriptions overlapped with existing municipal employees; and revise contracts to reflect each JO’s daily rate.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality studied manpower needs; redundant JO workers whose duties overlapped with municipal employees were terminated; contracts were revised to reflect daily rates and JO hiring procedures strengthened.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO and/or the official responsible for accepting deliveries of procured consumable items to submit the AIR to COA within 24 hours from acceptance.
observations.managementAction: The GSO promptly executed the recommendation; AIR copies for procured medicines were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, including the determination of the appropriate mode of disposal, appraisal of the same to prevent further deterioration and reduction of the properties’ economic value.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality disposed some unserviceable motor vehicles last May 2023. However, the remaining items subject to disposal were not addressed within the same year as the Inventory Committee was focused on the One-time Cleansing process and documentation. Moreover, the Municipality disposed the unserviceable properties amounting to P4,105,240.00 this May 2024. Also, upon checking the status of the Closed-circuit Television (CCTV), Smart Television, Projector, and White Board totaling P2,844,053.20 it was found that all the said units have been repaired and operational. Additionally, the Municipality is scheduling the next disposal of the remaining unserviceable properties in the Second Quarter of CY 2024. However, despite best efforts, one unit of a white Mitsubishi L300 FB 2003 model and one unit of a black Ford Everest 2014 model will not be subject to public auction due to missing documents. The GSO has exhausted all efforts to locate the documents but to no avail.
observations.reason: The Management has an ongoing inspection and verification of all the properties.
