ensure that all transactions of the Municipality are properly recorded using the
accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
properly record the purchases of welfare goods and medical supplies under the
account Welfare Goods for Distribution and Medical, Dental, and Laboratory
Supplies Inventory and the distributed/used portion to Welfare Goods Expenses
and Medical, Dental, and Laboratory Supplies Expense, respectively, which will be
closed out to the Transfer from General Fund of Unspent DRRMF account at the
end of the year for proper monitoring of receipts, issuances, and balances of
goods procured out of the prior year’s unexpended balances of LDRRMF.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and subsequently
record corresponding adjusting entry to recognize in the General Fund the
Medical Equipment worth P110,000.00, including the corresponding depreciation
expense.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
require the OIC-General Services Officer to:
a) exert effort to look for the relevant Deeds of Sale, Certificate of Registration
and other documents that will facilitate the transfer of the motor vehicles in
its name and the issuance of plates of heavy equipment;
b) follow-up with the LTO for the issuance of red plates to vehicles bearing
conduction sticker; and
c) expedite the registration of 14 motor vehicles with the Land Transportation
Office and their insurance coverage so that the Municipality will be
compensated in case of damage or loss.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC- General Services
Officer to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for nine unserviceable
motor vehicles and dispose the property in accordance with existing rules and
regulations on disposal. After which, it is recommended that the Municipal
Accountant drop the disposed property from the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
require all Accountable Officers to adhere strictly to the provisions of COA
Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of
cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and
other concerned officials to ensure that all projects and activities in the Annual
Investment Program for the 20 per cent Development Fund are implemented
accordingly to ensure that the socio-economic, environmental, and other benefits
intended to be derived from the projects are optimally enjoyed by the
Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Chairman of the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) submit to the Audit Team within 10 days from receipt
hereof a written explanation why the amount of P3,050,000.00 or part thereof paid
by the Municipality in CY 2020 for Consultancy Services should not be disallowed
in audit notwithstanding the following:
a) The procurement thereof was not in accordance with Annex H, Section V, D(7)
of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic
Act (RA) 9184;
b) Several consultants performed duties which were not highly technical and/or
could be performed by incumbent officials;
c) The Municipality did not submit proof that its own officials and employees did
not have the capability, capacity and/or time to undertake the subject services.
d) Hiring of three lawyers and one law firm was without prior concurrence of the
COA, in violation of COA Circular No. 95-011.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to submit the accomplishment reports to substantiate the payments
made totaling P1,070,000.00 and hereafter ensure that all accomplishment reports
are properly documented and attached on the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a) Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to submit explanation/justification,
for further evaluation of the Audit Team, on why the administrative expenses of
the Alfonso District Office and the expenses incurred for the landscaping of
Bilog Elementary School were charged to the SEF, considering also that the
observation had already been discussed with Management in prior year’s
audit, otherwise the subject transactions will be disallowed in audit; and
b) ALS Coordinators and District Supervisors to refund the honoraria and
consultant fees they received from the Municipality.

We commended Management for its full compliance on the receipt and timely
distribution of the subsidies to the identified eligible families within the
Municipality. However, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the
MSWDO to:
a) Submit evidence to account the refund made by three beneficiaries;
b) Investigate further and provide proof to substantially contradict the
allegations of six beneficiaries that they did not receive the full amount of P6,500.00; and
c) Coordinate with concerned Barangay officials to facilitate the submission to the COA
of the updated contact numbers of beneficiaries, for the Audit Team’s further or
expanded confirmation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee
to explain/justify the material discrepancies between the prices of the basic
commodities procured by the City in response to the COVID-19 and their
suggested retail prices issued by the authorized government agencies during the
price freeze implementation, to prevent suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat to submit
to the Audit Team the justifications on its inability to comply with the posting
requirement under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020, for further evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Service Officer to
submit to the Auditor samples or photographs and the amended Acceptance and
Inspection Reports indicating therein the complete specifications and brand
names, if any, of the delivered PPEs to facilitate price verification and technical
review of the reasonableness of the contract costs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the disbursement
vouchers and its supporting documents to the Commission on Audit with an
aggregate amount of P3,875,074.88 to avoid possible suspension/disallowance in
audit. Henceforth, ensure that all accounts are submitted within the first ten days
of the ensuing month as required under Section 7.2 of the Commission on Audit
RRSA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the OIC, General Services Office to reconcile the discrepancy of P10,187,257.09 between the Accounting records and RPCPPE and make the necessary adjustments. We also recommended that the General Services Office maintain property cards for property, plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of records from previous years are still on-going. GSO records decreased from P10,187,257.09 to P1,613,154.31. The Municipal Accountant also pointed out that there was already a significant improvement as the discrepancy between the Accounting and GSO records decreased from the reported variance of P10,187,257.09 in 2019 to P1,613,154.31 in 2020 through the concerted efforts of the Accounting and General Services Office.
observations.reason: Discrepancy between the Accounting and GSO records decreased from the reported variance of P10,187,257.09 in 2019 to P1,613,154.31 in 2020 through the concerted efforts of the Accounting and General Services Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant a complete and updated certified list showing the names of taxpayers with the amount of taxes due and collectible based on the RPT Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Cards to enable the latter to set up the receivables at the beginning of the year, to establish with certainty the reported RPT and SET Receivables as of year-end. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a) make necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balance of delinquent RPT and SET Receivables as of December 31, 2020; and b) henceforth, ensure that the financial transactions and operations of the Municipality are recorded in conformity with generally accepted accounting principles and in accordance with pertinent laws and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer committed to maintain and regularly submit the certified list of taxpayers for the ensuing years not later than June 30 of each year. The Municipal Accountant informed us that the adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of delinquent RPT and SET Receivables as of December 31, 2020 were made under Journal Entry Voucher Nos. 100-2021-05-3014 and 200-0-02885 both dated May 14, 2021. She also assured us that setting of receivables will be in conformity with generally accepted accounting principles and other pertinent rules and regulations.
observations.reason: The submitted list was not yet completed and updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) cause the reversion of the P762,113.10 refunded the total amount of P762,113.10 to the different government agencies in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013; and b) effect the necessary adjusting entries to record transactions amounting to P2,962,718.65 to their appropriate liability and income accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management refunded the total amount of P762,113.10 to the different government agencies after failing to locate the needed documents. As of CY 2020, only balances remained on the account. For the CYs 2018 and 2019 funded projects, implementation of the projects were completed in 2021. The adjusting entries were made under JEV Nos. 100-2020-07-29779, 300-2020-07-01757, 300-2020-07-01758, 300-2020-07-01759, and 300-2020-07-01760 dated July 28, 2020 to correct the errors noted by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The Management had funded projects; implementation of the projects were completed in 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. make the necessary entries to adjust the erroneous entries made in the Accounts Payable; b. exercise prudence in recording or accounting for transactions, specifically those involving Accounts Payable, to minimize, if not totally prevent, errors, to ensure fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements; and c. refrain from entrusting to contractors/suppliers and other private parties government disbursements and from recording Accounts Payable unless all supporting documents that will prove that there are actual deliveries of goods or services or work accomplishments are attached to the claims.
observations.managementAction: The Management made the necessary adjusting entries to correct the account on July 28 and 29, 2020. As of December 31, 2020, most of the transactions included in the account were for 2020. Accruals outstanding as of December 31, 2020 are now supported with documents necessary to complete government documentations. Documents were also no longer being entrusted to private parties.
observations.reason: Accruals outstanding as of December 31, 2020 are now supported with documents necessary to complete government documentations. Documents were also no longer being entrusted to private parties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to require all Accountable Officers to adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that the Municipality had only one Disbursing Officer, hence simultaneous events and transactions caused overlapping of cash advances. Similarly, documents pending with the event organizers and late claiming of allowance/pay-out of employees caused delays in the liquidation of cash advances. She also informed that additional Special Disbursing Officer will be appointed to avoid the overlapping of cash advances. Furthermore, she also explained that the Management already approved salary automation that might take effect next calendar year, hence no cash advances will be required for the payroll of permanent employees.
observations.reason: The Municipality still had only one disbursing officer; overlapping events and delayed supporting documents caused continued delays in liquidation though measures (additional SDO, salary automation) were planned.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-General Services Officer to: a) exert effort to look for the relevant Deeds of Sale, Certificate of Registration and other documents that will facilitate the transfer of the motor vehicles in its name and the issuance of plates of heavy equipment; b) follow-up with the LTO for the issuance of red plates to vehicles bearing conduction sticker; and c) expedite the registration of 14 motor vehicles with the Land Transportation Office and their insurance coverage so that the Municipality will be compensated in case of damage or loss. We also recommended that the OIC, General Services Officer prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for nine unserviceable motor vehicles and dispose the property in accordance with existing rules and regulations on disposal. After which, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant drop the disposed property from the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: In her letter dated June 3, 2021, the OIC – GSO replied that: • they have conducted a thorough search for the relevant documents that will facilitate the issuance of plates of the heavy equipment but to no avail. Efforts are still underway to look for these documents; • they were able to locate the certificates of the vehicles with conduction stickers and were already following-up with LTO for the issuance of red plates; • they were able to register some of the vehicles noted in the previous year’s audit and were still coordinating with the LTO for the registration of other vehicles. The Disposal Committee was also in the process of disposing unserviceable vehicles. The list of vehicles for disposal was already posted in public places. The target date of submission of initial documents was at the end of June 2021.
observations.reason: There were still unregistered vehicles and vehicles still using conduction stickers; additional vehicles were included in the list of unregistered vehicles for CY 2020; most of these vehicles were either under repair or already for disposal and the disposal process was still underway.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit to the Audit Team within 10 days from receipt hereof a written explanation why the amount or part thereof paid by the Municipality for Consultancy Services should not be disallowed in audit notwithstanding that: a) The procurement thereof was not in accordance with Annex H, Section V, D(7) of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184; b) Several consultants performed duties which were not highly technical and/or could be performed by incumbent officials; c) The Municipality did not submit proof that its own officials and employees did not have the capability, capacity and/or time to undertake the subject services; d) Hiring of three lawyers and one law firm was without prior concurrence of the COA, in violation of COA Circular No. 95-011. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the accomplishment reports to substantiate the payments made totaling P1,070,000.00 and hereafter ensure that all accomplishment reports are properly documented and attached on the disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction: In the submitted Minutes of the Meeting held on June 15, 2021 attended by the concerned Municipal Officials, it was agreed that: a) The regularity and necessity of hiring the consultants were indeed not firmly established. Given the situation, the body resolved to recommend to the Local Chief Executive the immediate termination of contract of all existing consultants; b) The corresponding proof of rescissions of their contracts will be submitted to the Audit Team; c) Rehiring of consultant shall henceforth be based on the procedural guidelines prescribed in Annex H Section V D(7) of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act (RA) 9184; and d) The written concurrence of COA prior to the hiring or employment of a private lawyer if such services are inevitable and extremely necessary will be secured. The Municipal Accountant also submitted the Accomplishment Reports on June 15, 2021.
observations.reason: Management's responses are prospective and did not justify the expenses incurred in CY 2020. The Accomplishment Reports submitted will be examined and validated by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to ensure that all projects and activities in the Annual Investment Program for the 20 per cent Development Fund are implemented accordingly to ensure that the socio-economic, environmental, and other benefits intended to be derived from the projects are optimally enjoyed by the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer explained that the construction of water system at Barangay Luksuhin Ilaya was under the supervision of Alfonso Waterworks Office and that the Municipality waited for the completion of the construction of deep well funded by the Provincial Government of Cavite which will provide for the definite water source.
observations.reason: For CY 2020, the Municipality still had development projects not implemented (one project with total allocation of P4,500,000.00 was not implemented).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to follow the format of the LDRRMFIP prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and that the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations (RAAO) for LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The submitted LDRRMFIP was already in accordance with the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for proper evaluation and monitoring of the Municipality's DRRM programs, projects and activities. The Municipal Budget Officer maintained separate RAAO for LDRRMF.
observations.reason: The Municipal Budget Officer already maintained separate RAAO for LDRRMF as prescribed in Section 5.1.4 of the same COA Circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for GAD to monitor all GAD-related expenditures. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned municipal official/s to adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 on the timely submission of GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) to the COA Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office created separate subsidiary ledgers for GAD to monitor all GAD-related expenditures. The Municipal Mayor was advised to ensure concerned officials adhere to COA Circular No. 2014-001. The GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report were submitted on May 11, 2021, upon request of the Auditor.
observations.reason: GAD Plan and Budget and the GAD Accomplishment Report were submitted beyond the prescribed period; they were submitted on May 11, 2021 upon request of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant and the OIC, GSO to reconcile the discrepancy of
P10,187,257.09 between the Accounting records and RPCPPE and make the

We also recommended that the General Services Office maintain property cards

a) stop the practice of crediting the difference of the total debits and total

Receivables without any basis; and
b) henceforth, ensure that the financial transactions and operations of the
Municipality are recorded in conformity with generally accepted accounting

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) cause the reversion of the P762,113.10 unexpended balance of fund
transfers to the National Government in compliance with COA Circular
No. 94-013; and
b) effect the necessary adjusting entries to record transactions amounting to
P2,962,718.65 to their appropriate liability and income accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. make the necessary entries to adjust the erroneous entries made in the
Accounts Payable;
b. exercise prudence in recording or accounting for transactions, specifically
involving Accounts Payable, to minimize, if not totally prevent errors, to
ensure fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements; and

documents necessary to support government disbursements and from

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-General Services
Officer to:
a. exert effort to look for the relevant Deeds of Sale, Certificate of Registration
and other documents that will facilitate the transfer of the motor vehicles in
its name and the issuance of plates of heavy equipment;
b. follow-up with the LTO for the issuance of red plates to vehicles bearing
conduction sticker; and
c. expedite the registration of 14 motor vehicles with the Land Transportation
Office and their insurance coverage so that the Municipality will be
compensated in case of damage or loss.

We further recommended that the OIC, General Services Office initiate the process
of disposing the eight unserviceable motor vehicles by preparing Inventory and
Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and the Waste Material Report and
complying with the other documentary and procedural requirements for disposal
of unserviceable properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Chairman of the BAC and other concerned officers to provide
justification, for further evaluation of the Audit Team, on why the
Municipality failed to comply with the procedural and substantive
requirements in the hiring of 14 consultants as provided under the IRR of
RA 9184, otherwise the consultancy services expenses amounting to
P1,480,000.00 will be suspended and/or disallowed in audit;
b. provide written justification/explanation, together with supporting
documents, for evaluation of the Audit Team on:
i. why the Municipality entered into a Contract of Consultancy Services on
Press Relations with a person who did not have sufficient experience and therefore, established expertise in the field of press relations,
otherwise all payments to her will be suspended and/or disallowed in
audit;
ii. why the Consultant on Optometry rendered various services to the
Municipality which were totally unrelated or foreign to the field of
optometry, contrary to the terms of her contract, otherwise all payments
to her will be suspended and/or disallowed in audit;
iii. why the Consultant for Barangay Affairs was expected to deliver
optometry services per her contract with the Municipality and why her
reported accomplishments such as photocopying of documents,
accompanying the Municipal Mayor in his daily activities, conducting
adventure shootings, were considered as highly-technical in nature,
otherwise all payments to her will be suspended and/or disallowed in
audit;
iv. why a Consultant for Health and Wellness was hired as her reported
accomplishments such as encouraging people to work out and
educating them on the benefits of having a healthy lifestyle could
already be done by the employees/medical staff at the Rural Health Unit
and why she was not able to submit proof that she had conducted
zumba classes, otherwise all payments to her will be suspended and/or
disallowed in audit;
v. why the Municipality did not obtain the written concurrence of the
Commission on Audit prior to hiring four Legal Consultants and why all
of them rendered identical services to the Municipality, otherwise all
payments to them will be suspended and/or disallowed in audit.
c. direct the Human Resource Management Officer to clearly define the works
or activities which could only be accomplished through hiring of
consultants, incorporate them in the contracts of the consultants and use
them as bases to justify their hiring and the amount to be paid for their
services; and
d. henceforth ensure that the procedural requirements in the hiring of
consultants provided under the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9182, particularly the
responsibility of the BAC to negotiate with the consultants, validate their
qualifications, and recommend their hiring to the Municipal Mayor, are
strictly complied with.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and
other concerned officials to ensure that all projects and activities in the Annual
Investment Program for the 20 per cent Development Fund are implemented
accordingly to ensure that the socio-economic, environmental and other benefits
intended to be derived from the projects are optimally enjoyed by the
Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer to follow the format of the LDRRMFIP
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for the proper
evaluation and monitoring of the Municipality’s DRRM programs, projects and
activities.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget
Officer to maintain separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations
for LDRRMF as prescribed in Section 5.1.4 of the same COA Circular.

We recommended that the Accounting Department maintain separate subsidiary
ledgers for GAD expenditures that will facilitate the audit/validation and reporting
of fund utilization.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned Municipal
official/s to strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18,
2014 on the timely submission of GAD Plan and Budget (GPB and GAD
Accomplishment Report (AR) to the COA Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a) Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to submit
explanation/justification, for further evaluation of the Audit Team, why the
administrative expenses of the Alfonso District Office were charged
against the Special Education Fund, and/or submit a certification from
concerned official of the DepEd Division Office that the District Office had
no allocation from the DepED to cover the subject expenses, otherwise,
the subject transactions shall be disallowed in audit;
b) Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to submit
explanation/justification, for further evaluation of the Audit Team, why the
two teachers who had travel authorities on official time only were allowed
to claim traveling and other expenses, otherwise the amount shall be
disallowed in audit; and
c) Bids and Award Committee to submit explanation/justification, for further
evaluation of the Audit Team, why the expenses relative to the conduct of
simultaneous cluster meet were procured through shopping and small
value procurement even though the amounts involved exceeded the
threshold provided for the alternative methods of procurement, otherwise
the expenses shall be suspended and/or disallowed in audit.
It is also recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise concerned school
officials to ensure that requests for items or funds needed for the regular
programs/activities of the DepED, which are to be charged against the SEF,
are submitted to responsible or concerned Municipal Office early enough to give the
BAC reasonable and sufficient lead time to prepare for the conduct of public
bidding.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all concerned
officials to ensure that the Special Education Fund is only utilized for purposes
provided under Section 272 of RA No. 7160 as implemented by DepEd-DBM-DILG
Joint Circular No. 01 dated January 19, 2017 and that all procurements of the
Municipality are in accordance with the IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the OIC, GSO to reconcile the discrepancy of P20,305,549.08 between the Accounting records and the RPCPPE and make the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant checked and approved the validity of the mentioned documents. The Municipal Accountant provided some of the required supporting documents attached. In CY 2020, accounts balances were cleaned-up.
observations.reason: There is non-availability of records particularly the lapsing schedules. Further the Municipal Accountant instructed the staff handling monitoring of lapsing schedule to dig the history of the balances and to find the data of the previous year’s records. It is assessed that majority of the difference are coming from the previous years. Reiterated in Observation No. 1, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation of records with the LBP-Dasmariñas Branch to identify the reason of discrepancy and make adjustment to accounts, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: JEV was prepared reflecting adjustment to align the loans payable with the amount reflected in the Land Bank. The Land Bank amortization table was also obtained during 2019 year-end closing to tally the balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancy in the Due to and Due from LGU accounts of the Municipality with its reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province and make the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Aligned and tallied balances with the Provincial accounting books. Copies of Journal Entry Vouchers and Subsidiary Ledgers were obtained from the Province.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a) Provide all the required supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation; and b) ensure that all claims are supported with complete documents before they are processed for payment to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant checked and approved the validity of the mentioned documents. The Municipal Accountant provided some of the required supporting documents attached. In CY 2020, accounts balances were already based on actual incurrence of obligations to document fund utilization.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant provided some of the supporting documents. There were still missing supporting evidences; hence not yet 100 percent complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to cause the reversion of the unexpended balance of fund transfers to the National Government in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013 and EO.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor planned and instructed the Municipal Accountant to cause the reversion of the unexpended balance of fund transfers to the National Government. The Municipal Accountant instructed the concerned department heads to use the fund for the project and if till November, the fund will not be used, they will return the fund to the Government.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 3, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that all claims are supported with complete documentation before they are processed for payment to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant provided some of the required supporting documents. We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that all claims are supported with complete documents before they are processed for payment to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a) grant cash advances for travels based on duly approved itinerary of travel; and b) require strict adherence to the timely settlement and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured that cash advances for travel will be granted on duly approved itinerary of travel. The Municipal Accountant ensured that cash advances are fully liquidated and settled at the end of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing cash advances to cash advances employees/accountable officers with outstanding previous cash advances; b) ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year; and c) limit the maximum cash accountability of the Disbursing Officer within the allowable maximum cash accountability covered by the bond pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and BTr Circular No. 02-2009. Otherwise, she should be authorized to apply for an increase in the coverage of her bond commensurate to her actual cash accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured that all cash advances are fully liquidated and settled at the end of the year. The Disbursing Officer had already increased her accountability.
observations.reason: Cash advances were still not settled within the prescribed period. The timely settlement and liquidation of all cash advances were still not strictly practiced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to: a) Maintain an updated Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations and record therein appropriations, allotments received and the obligations incurred against said allotments; and b) draw Obligation Requests based on actual incurrence of obligations to document fund utilization.
observations.managementAction: The Budget Officer maintained updated RAAO and commented that they adapted the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to fully utilize the LDRRMF and strictly for DRRM PPAs only.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive had already directed the MDRRMO to fully utilize the LDRRMF and strictly for DRRM PPAs only. The refund from General Fund to Trust fund was already transferred on August 8, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to ensure that all projects and activities in the AIP for the 20 per cent Development fund are implemented accordingly to ensure that the socio-economic, environmental and other benefits are enjoyed by the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management already had: a. 13 projects amounting to P7,015,000.00 fully implemented; b. Five projects amounting to P890,000.00 were reprogrammed and used for covid related expenses. Sanggunian Bayan Resolutions were submitted for the realigned or reprogrammed projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

accounts payable and henceforth, recognize liabilities and/or claims based
on valid and adequate documentary evidences; and
(b) Provide valid explanation and supporting documents to the adjustments
made to Accounts Payable totaling P649,148.65.

cause the reversion of the P535,205.45 unexpended balance of fund transfers to the
National Government in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013 and EO.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
secure the above-mentioned lacking documents from the Bids and Awards

of the Auditor for evaluation.

claims are supported with complete documentation before they are processed for
payment to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor –

Itinerary of Travel; and

advances for travel.

employees/accountable officers with outstanding previous cash advance/s;

No. 97-002 regarding the timely settlement and liquidation of cash advances.

the grant of cash advances to the Disbursing Officer within the allowable maximum
cash accountability covered by the bond pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and
BTr Circular No. 02-2009. Otherwise, she should be authorized to apply for an
increase in the coverage of her bond commensurate to her actual cash accountability.

Obligations and record therein appropriations, allotments received and
the obligations incurred against said allotments; and

document fund utilization.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the members of the BAC and the OIC- GSO to coordinate with concerned school officials to remind them of their obligation to submit their respective PPMPs and ensure that these are consolidated in the Municipality’s APP. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipality stop the practice of splitting of purchases and pursue competitive bidding in all its procurement unless resort to shopping or other alternative modes of procurement is warranted by the conditions set forth by the IRR of RA 9184. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Chairman of the BAC, the OIC- GSO and other concerned officials to provide the Audit Team with an updated Acceptance and Inspec- tion Reports (AIR) with detailed specifications of the televisions purchased to prevent the suspension and/or disallowance in audit of the subject transactions. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor order said officials to ensure that all procurement documents have complete specifications of the goods/services to be purchased.

PPMPs had been prepared by schools and consolidated in the APP.

Good/services procured during the year had complete specifications.
